Question: Can I record this? I'd use anonymized quotes in investor materials — name/company omitted by default; title/role OK unless you say otherwise.
Quick setup — your role, team size, and roughly what % of your work involves AI tools right now.
Tell me about a recent AI session you wish was still open — or that you had to restart from scratch. What happened? What got lost?
Who on your team knows what you just did with AI? How does that knowledge travel across people — or does it?
Do Claude Projects, ChatGPT memory, Cursor rules, or a shared Notion already cover this for you? Where does it break?
Does your team have a *named* process for AI-assisted work? What do you call it? (Listen for: "not really", "kind of Agile", "we're figuring it out".)
Walk me through a time your existing process — Agile, sprints, Shape Up, whatever — felt *off* because AI moved faster (or slower) than the rhythm it was built for.

If I said 'Fish model — Head explores, Body builds, Tail ships, and every task is sized bigger-vs-smaller × known-vs-unknown' — does any part of that vocabulary map to something missing in your world?
Is Agile-with-AI basically fine for you, just faster? What would have to change for you to switch frameworks?
Walk me through the last AI tool you tried to install on your work machine. What happened?
When something AI-powered lands on your machine, what runs through your head — permissions, data, what it's watching, where things go?
Does anyone on your team use personal Claude or ChatGPT accounts for work? Why? (Ask casually — surfaces shadow AI without judgment.)
Is install actually the blocker, or is it something else — time, priority, permission from IT?

Section: The pain you'd pay to fix
Question: What pain in your AI workflow would you pay — actual money, not 'I'd try it' — to make go away?

Section: The pain you'd pay to fix
Question: What should I have asked that I didn't? What AI-workflow pain did none of my questions touch?
Ask for 1 referral — one design leader or hands-on practitioner who should be in this research.
Confirm anonymization preferences — name/company omitted, title/role OK, or fully anonymous quotes.
